Petition For Surcharge, Removal Of Administrator, Return Of Estate Property, Double Damages, And Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Jan-12-2026 Continued To Mar-25-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Dec-26-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas Wrobel for petitioners Tiana Hightower-Blunt and Teri Hightower, who were also present (via Zoom).
Jennifer Hightower, administrator in pro per.

The matter was called for hearing on 1) Petition for Removal of Personal Representative; and 2) Petition for Letters of Administration.

Jennifer Hightower stated her opposition to the petitions, disputed the allegations, and requested to remain administrator. She argued that removal was premature and requested a waiver or reduction of the previously ordered bond due to financial hardship and difficulty obtaining bonding.

Mr. Wrobel reported that service of the citation had been completed and argued that, despite the passage of approximately eleven months, Ms. Hightower had been unable to obtain a bond or have letters issued, resulting in the estate remaining dormant. He raised concerns regarding potential access to estate assets prior to administration and noted that related proceedings addressing those issues were pending. Mr. Wrobel requested removal of Ms. Hightower and issuance of letters of administration to Tiana Hightower-Blunt.

The Court stated that the estate could not proceed without a bonded personal representative and that further delay was not appropriate.

The Court took both petitions under submission and indicated its intent to grant the petition for removal of Jennifer Hightower as personal representative and the petition for letters of administration filed by Tiana Hightower-Blunt, subject to submission of proposed orders by Mr. Wrobel.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-26-2025 Granted Per Written Order

Petition For Removal Of Personal Representative
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Continued To Dec-15-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Thomas Wrobel, for Teri Hightower and Tiana Hightower-Blunt, Petitioners/Objectors.
Srijana Dhakhwa, for Jennifer Hightower, Administrator/Petitioner.

At the outset, the court queries Ms. Dhakhwa regarding Letters not being issued as of the calling of this matter. Counsel reports that her client has had issues obtaining a bond. Mr. Wrobel advises that his client qualifies to obtain a bond.

After discussion regarding the preceding, the court advises that it will allow Mr. Wrobel to obtain a citation to serve on Administrator Jennifer Hightower, without an ex parte application.

The subject matter is continued to Dec-15-2025 at 9:00 AM in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Robles. Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved Of Counsel
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Continued To Sep-24-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Thomas S. Wrobel for Tiana Hightower-Blunt, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Srijana Dhakhwa for and with Jennifer Hightower, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Bridget Hightower Morris, Teri Hightower are also present via Zoom.

Ms. Dhakhwa argued that Jennifer Hightower, as the spouse, holds statutory priority for appointment as administrator. Mr. Wrobel raised concerns about Jennifer Hightower's treatment of the decedent during his final months, citing allegations of neglect, and asserted that these issues demonstrate her unsuitability to serve as administrator.

The court took the matter under submission for further review and indicated that a decision on the competing petitions would be made.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
